step_by_step_thinking:
Complete denture teeth being in edge to edge contact in centric occlusion implies that the cusps of the upper and lower teeth are aligning properly to distribute load evenly. Given the context of achieving stable and functional occlusion, option C seems applicable because it ensures that during the closing cycle, the inclination of the cusp guides the movement correctly – specifically, the upper cusp moving buccally and the lower cusp moving lingually supports balanced occlusal loading.
\boxed{C} Based on the principles discussed regarding the occlusal anatomy and guiding mechanisms, grinding the cusps appropriately as described in option C would facilitate proper guidance and prevent undue stress on specific regions of the dentures.
